text:
ATLANTA, Ga. (Atlanta News First) - As the world gathers in Paris for the 2024 Olympics, Saturday marked a grim anniversary here in Atlanta.

It was the 28th anniversary of the bombing that shook the opening ceremony at the 1996 Olympic Games.

Eric Rudolph detonated a bomb at Centennial Olympic Park, killing one woman and injuring more than 100 others.

The attack has left a scar on many people in Atlanta, like Kimberly Krautter, who was working near the blast.

“Everybody thought--who wasn’t immediately where the tower was--thought it was fireworks,” Krautter said. “But when you heard the screams, and the scattering and the chaos, my first thought was my crew.”

Krautter tells us all of her crew made it out okay.

Eric Rudolph was captured in North Carolina seven years after the bombing. He confessed to three more bombings: abortion clinics in Sandy Springs and Birmingham, Alabama and the lesbian bar Otherside Lounge of Atlanta.

He’s currently serving four consecutive life sentences at a maximum security prison in Colorado.

title: Remembering the Centennial Olympic Park bombing at the 1996 Olympics
description: The Atlanta Olympics were marred by a bombing during the opening ceremony.
keywords: centennial olympic park,centennial olympic park atlanta,centennial olympic park bombing,eric rudolph,atlanta olympics,1996 olympics,1996 summer olympics,atlanta news first,atlanta news,anf,wanf
